blockingIssues.description: `TestSameWorkspaceUserACannotCreateTimeEntryAsUserB` and `TestSameWorkspaceUserACannotContinueUserBEntryViaCreate` still tolerate successful unauthorized create/continue requests, and the fix never adds direct HTTP/bootstrap proof that the real `POST /time_entries` boundary denies the request.

The original read-isolation regression only exercised owner-scoped service calls (`GetCurrentTimeEntry`, `ListTimeEntries`, `ListUserTimeEntries`) and did not prove that attacker-visible HTTP tracking read surfaces deny cross-user reads.

The read-surface fix added attacker-facing HTTP proof but still missed owner-still-can-read history or entry-by-id assertions in the same seeded scenario, leaving the history half under-proven until the later owner HTTP readback fix.
The restart/continue fix modeled restart via `UpdateTimeEntry(... Stop=nil)` even though that service path preserves stop values and is not the documented continue/create boundary.

The original write-isolation feature omitted the continue/create mutation surface, so same-workspace attackers still lacked a proven denial path for creating or continuing another user's entry until a later fix feature.
